DECLARATION OF DAMIEN P. LILLIS IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION (TRANSACTION ID # 59920159) FILED BY DEFENDANT HARLOW, DANIEL INDIVIDUALLY VIRTUAL CONSTRUCTION, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS VIRCON, INC.)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DANIEL HARLOW AND VIRTUAL CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DOES NOT DISPUTE THE EXISTENCE OF A BINDING ARBITRATION AGREEMENT BUT INSTEAD ARGUES ITS CLAIMS ARE NOT ARBITRABLE AND DEFENDANTS WAIVED THEIR RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. THE "REMEDIES UPON DEFAULT" CLAUSE OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT DOES NOT CREATE AN EXCEPTION ALLOWING WELLS FARGO TO AVOID ARBITRATION. BECAUSE THE MASTER AGREEMENT PROVIDES FOR THE AAA COMMERCIAL ARBITRATION RULES WHICH SPECIFIES THAT THE ARBITRATOR DECIDES ARBITRABILITY, THE ARBITRATOR, NOT THE COURT, SHOULD DECIDE ARBITRABILITY ISSUES. (DREAM THEATER, INC. V. DREAM THEATER (2004) 124 CAL.APP.4TH 547, 557.) IT IS IMPRACTICABLE AT THIS STAGE IN LITIGATION TO REQUIRE DEFENDANTS TO PROCEED WITH AN INTERNAL RESOLUTION PROCESS PRIOR TO ARBITRATION. NOR IS THERE IS ANY BASIS TO DETERMINE THAT DEFENDANTS WAIVED THEIR RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE COURT MAY RULE ON THE BANK'S MOTION PURSUANT TO THE "NO WAIVER; PROVISIONAL REMEDIES" SECTION IN THE MASTER AGREEMENT, THE MOTION FAILS AS A MATTER OF BOTH PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY. PROCEDURALLY, THE BANK'S MOTION IS AN IMPERMISSIBLE EFFORT TO OBTAIN A PREJUDGMENT ATTACHMENT WITHOUT MEETING THE REQUIREMENTS FOR AN ATTACHMENT. (DOYKA V. SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 233 CAL. APP. 3D 1134). WEST COAST CONSTRUCTION CO. V. OCEANO SANITARY DISTRICT (1971) 17 CAL. APP. 3D 693 IS INAPPOSITE BECAUSE, AS STATED IN DOYKA, THE INJUNCTION IN WEST COAST "RESTRAINED USE OF IDENTIFIABLE ASSETS FOR SPECIFIC PURPOSES," AND THE BANK'S REQUESTED INJUNCTION IS NOT SO LIMITED. SUBSTANTIVELY, THE BANK HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY THE PARTIES ON THE MERITS OF THEIR DISPUTE IS EVEN BALANCED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Harlow And Virtual Construction, Inc. To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Wu and Melissa Black of Squire Patton Boggs telephone number 213-689-5115 for Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank.
Damien Lillis of Lillis Pitha LLP telephone number 415-814-0405 for Defendants Virtual Construction, Inc. and Daniel Harlow.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendants Daniel Harlow And Virtual Construction, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Action is granted. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. does not dispute the existence of a binding arbitration agreement but instead argues its claims are not arbitrable and defendants waived their right to arbitrate. The "Remedies upon Default" clause of the Master Agreement does not create an exception allowing Wells Fargo to avoid arbitration. Because the Master Agreement provides for the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules which specifies that the arbitrator decides arbitrability, the arbitrator, not the court, should decide arbitrability issues. (Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557.) It is impracticable at this stage in litigation to require defendants to proceed with an internal resolution process prior to arbitration. Nor is there is any basis to determine that defendants waived their right to arbitrate. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-12-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Of Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Wu and Melissa Black of Squire Patton Boggs telephone number 213-689-5115 for Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank.
Damien Lillis of Lillis Pitha LLP telephone number 415-814-0405 for Defendants Virtual Construction, Inc. and Daniel Harlow.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Although the court may rule on the Bank's motion pursuant to the "No Waiver; Provisional Remedies" section in the Master Agreement, the motion fails as a matter of both procedurally and substantively. Procedurally, the Bank's motion is an impermissible effort to obtain a prejudgment attachment without meeting the requirements for an attachment. (Doyka v. Superior Court (1991) 233 Cal. App. 3d 1134). West Coast Construction Co. v. Oceano Sanitary District (1971) 17 Cal. App. 3d 693 is inapposite because, as stated in Doyka, the injunction in West Coast "restrained use of identifiable assets for specific purposes," and the Bank's requested injunction is not so limited. Substantively, the Bank has not shown that it is likely to prevail on the merits. The evidence submitted by the parties on the merits of their dispute is even balanced. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
